memes.label: Allington
memes.description: Country home of the Dale family near Guestwick, where Grace Crawley finds refuge during her father's trial, and the setting for Lily Dale's story that bridges the Barsetshire and Palliser series.

memes.label: Archdeacon Grantly
memes.description: The Archdeacon of Barchester, father of Major Grantly, a proud and powerful clergyman whose fierce opposition to his son's marriage drives much of the novel's domestic drama.

memes.label: Barchester
memes.description: The cathedral city and seat of the diocese of Barchester, the ecclesiastical and social centre of Barsetshire.

memes.label: Bishop Proudie
memes.description: The Bishop of Barchester, a well-meaning but weak-willed prelate whose authority is perpetually undermined by his domineering wife, Mrs. Proudie.

memes.label: Conway Dalrymple
memes.description: A fashionable young portrait painter in London, friend of John Eames, whose entanglement with Mrs. Dobbs Broughton and Clara Van Siever forms one of the novel's principal comic subplots.

memes.label: Dr. Tempest
memes.description: The rector of Silverbridge and rural dean who is tasked by Bishop Proudie with investigating the Crawley cheque case — a commission he executes with deliberate and infuriating slowness.

memes.label: Dr. Thorne
memes.description: A respected country doctor and gentleman of Chaldicotes who sits as a magistrate at the Crawley committal hearing and represents the humane, practical wisdom of the Barsetshire gentry.

memes.label: Ecclesiastical Politics in Barsetshire
memes.description: The system of church patronage, preferment, and diocesan power struggles that shapes the social and institutional world of the Barsetshire novels, centred on the cathedral city of Barchester.

memes.label: Framley
memes.description: Lady Lufton's estate and parish in Barsetshire, home to the Lufton family and a centre of county influence.

memes.label: Grace Crawley
memes.description: Eldest daughter of Josiah Crawley, whose love for Major Grantly and the scandal of her father's alleged crime form the emotional core of the novel.

memes.label: The Marriage of Grace Crawley and Major Grantly
memes.description: The courtship and marriage of Grace Crawley and Major Henry Grantly, a union that bridges the social chasm between the disgraced Crawley family and the aristocratic Grantlys of Plumstead.

memes.label: Hogglestock
memes.description: An impoverished parish in Barsetshire where Josiah Crawley serves as perpetual curate.

memes.label: John Eames
memes.description: A rising young clerk in London, known to friends as Johnny, whose long and persistent courtship of Lily Dale forms one of the central romantic threads of the Barsetshire chronicles.

memes.label: Josiah Crawley
memes.description: Perpetual curate of Hogglestock, a proud and impoverished clergyman falsely accused of stealing a cheque.

memes.label: Lady Lufton
memes.description: Matriarch of Framley Court, formidable dowager who exercises social and ecclesiastical influence across Barsetshire, and grandmother to the young Lord Lufton whose wife is an ally of Grace Crawley.

memes.label: Lily Dale
memes.description: Grace Crawley's closest friend and confidante in London, a young woman of firm resolution who has sworn never to marry after being jilted by Adolphus Crosbie.

memes.label: Lord Lufton
memes.description: The young peer of Framley Court whose twenty-pound cheque, sent charitably to Josiah Crawley, becomes the central piece of evidence in the cheque-forgery accusation.

memes.label: Major Grantly
memes.description: Son of Archdeacon Grantly, a widowed army officer who falls in love with Grace Crawley.

memes.label: Mrs. Crawley
memes.description: Wife of Josiah Crawley, the perpetual curate of Hogglestock, who endures extreme poverty with quiet dignity while managing the family's precarious finances.

memes.label: Mr. Chadwick
memes.description: The bishop's secretary and man of business at Barchester Palace, through whom Mrs. Proudie attempts to direct the ecclesiastical proceedings against Josiah Crawley — a functionary caught between episcopal authority and his own sense of what is proper.

memes.label: Mr. Harding
memes.description: The gentle former precentor of Barchester Cathedral, grandfather of Major Grantly, and the moral center of the Barsetshire series in his final years.

memes.label: Mr. Musselboro
memes.description: A cunning City man and business associate of Mr. Dobbs Broughton whose financial manipulations contribute to the Broughtons' ruin and whose distinctive whiskers serve as his most memorable feature.

memes.label: Mr. Walker
memes.description: The Silverbridge attorney who advises the Crawley family during the cheque-forgery case, acting as the crucial local intermediary between the impoverished clergyman and the legal system that threatens to destroy him.

memes.label: Mrs. Dobbs Broughton
memes.description: A fashionable London hostess whose romantic fascination with the painter Conway Dalrymple, and her husband's financial ruin, form one of the novel's main comic-melodramatic subplots.

memes.label: Mrs. Proudie
memes.description: The domineering wife of the Bishop of Barchester, a formidable figure in Barsetshire ecclesiastical politics.

memes.label: Poverty and Clerical Dignity
memes.description: The moral tension between material poverty and personal dignity as embodied by Josiah Crawley, a theme that explores how Victorian society measured a man's worth against his income and how a clergyman's honour could survive economic ruin.

memes.label: Silverbridge
memes.description: Parliamentary borough and market town in Barsetshire where Josiah Crawley is committed for trial, and home to the attorney Mr. Walker and the butcher whose disputed cheque triggers the novel's central conflict.

memes.label: The Chronicles of Barsetshire
memes.description: Anthony Trollope's series of six novels set in the fictional county of Barsetshire, published between 1855 and 1867, comprising a sustained portrait of Victorian clerical and provincial life.

memes.label: The Crawley Trial
memes.description: The legal proceedings against Josiah Crawley for the alleged theft of a twenty-pound cheque, the central plot event of the novel that tests the justice of Barsetshire's institutions and the honour of its principal characters.

memes.label: The Diocese of Barchester
memes.description: The ecclesiastical jurisdiction centred on Barchester Cathedral, comprising the parishes, clergy, and church institutions of Barsetshire, governed by the Bishop of Barchester and his administration.

memes.label: The Ecclesiastical Commission
memes.description: The body established in 1835 to reform the finances of the Church of England, whose work of redistributing church revenues forms the institutional backdrop to Josiah Crawley's poverty in The Last Chronicle of Barset.

memes.label: The Last Chronicle of Barset
memes.description: The sixth and final novel of Anthony Trollope's Chronicles of Barsetshire series, published in 1867, centred on the trial of Josiah Crawley for the alleged theft of a twenty-pound cheque.

memes.label: Mr. Toogood
memes.description: London solicitor of Gray's Inn, cousin to Mary Crawley, who takes up Josiah Crawley's defence in the cheque-forgery case and travels to Silverbridge to investigate.
